meta.title: Voter Flows
meta.subtitle: Primary Ballot to Runoffs and Favorability to Ballots
meta.sample: Modeled likely Republican primary voter estimates
meta.methodLabel: Calibrated bridge flows from modeled marginals

group_navigation.group_label: Louisiana Republican Primary for US Senate 2026

section_headers.ballot_flows_explainer: How primary-ballot supporters of each group move into the selected two-person runoff.
section_headers.candidate_navigation: Favorability ↔ Test Ballot Flows: Select Candidate and View

feedback_callout.heading: Share Your Feedback
feedback_callout.body: Have questions about these Louisiana voter-flow bridges or ideas for additional diagnostics? We would love to hear from you.

key_takeaways: In a Fleming vs Cassidy runoff, Fleming gets 3.2 percentage points more of the Letlow primary ballot voters than Cassidy does, however, the 28.7 pts are undecdied.
In a Letlow vs Cassidy runoff, Letlow gets 1.8 percentage points more of the Fleming primary ballot voters than Cassidy does, however, the 27.2 pts are undecided.
In a Fleming vs Letlow runoff, Fleming gets 16.1 percentage points more of the Cassidy primary ballot voters than Letlow does, however, the 33.1 pts are undecided.
methodology_note: Bridge flows are estimated by calibrating a joint distribution between modeled favorability and modeled ballot outcomes from the same survey wave. We start from a survey-based weighted seed table, apply light smoothing where needed, and then use iterative proportional fitting (IPF) so recovered totals match modeled marginals. This allows us to show both the overall size of each flow and the conditional composition of each source group. These bridge flows are calibrated synthetic estimates that reconcile modeled marginals, not raw respondent-level cross-tabs.

direction_modes.label: Favorability → Test Ballot
direction_modes.interpretation: Among voters in this favorability bucket, where do they go on the selected test ballot question?

direction_modes.label: Test Ballot → Favorability
direction_modes.interpretation: Among voters who choose each test ballot option, what favorability views do they hold toward the selected figure?
